research_series.series_name: Pain, Nociception, Central Sensitization, Fatigue, Mood, Sleep, Autonomic State, and Cognition

patterns.pattern_name: Chronic centralized pain is associated with impaired attention, executive function, working memory, and processing speed

mediator.name: attentional_capture_sleep_disturbance_affective_distress
mediator.pathway: pain-related cognitive load and sleep/affective disruption
mediator.mechanism: persistent pain consumes attentional resources and co-occurs with sleep disturbance, anxiety/depression, and fatigue

evidence.summary: Multiple human cross-sectional and cohort studies link chronic or centralized pain with deficits in attention, executive function, working memory, and processing speed, but causal direction and mediation are not fully resolved.

key_sources.title: Central Sensitization Severity in Chronic Low Back Pain: Associations with Sleep, Distress, Cortisol, and Visuospatial Working Memory
key_sources.relevant_finding: Central sensitization severity associated with poor sleep, distress, elevated morning cortisol, and reduced visuospatial working memory.

key_sources.title: Cognitive Impairments in Fibromyalgia Syndrome
key_sources.relevant_finding: Fibromyalgia associated with impairments across processing speed, attention, and memory domains, correlated with pain and affective factors.

patterns.pattern_name: Poor sleep and affective distress partially mediate pain-related fatigue and cognitive impairment

mediator.name: sleep_disturbance_and_affective_distress
mediator.pathway: pain disrupts sleep and increases catastrophizing/anxiety/depression, which worsens fatigue and cognitive symptoms
mediator.mechanism: bidirectional pain-sleep-fatigue loop and attentional/emotional load

evidence.summary: Mediation analyses indicate that sleep quality and affective factors partially mediate relationships between chronic pain/central sensitization and cognition/disability.

patterns.pattern_name: Autonomic dysfunction and baroreflex impairment are linked to fibromyalgia symptom burden

mediator.name: baroreflex_impairment_autonomic_dysfunction
mediator.mechanism: blunted cardiac/vasomotor/myocardial baroreflex sensitivity and reduced autonomic flexibility

evidence.summary: Physiological monitoring in fibromyalgia links blunted baroreflex sensitivity/effectiveness to pain, fatigue, mood, and sleep symptoms.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Central Sensitization Inventory alone proves nociceptive sensitization.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: The review notes CSI may reflect psychological hypervigilance more than true nociceptive gain.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Subjective fatigue in chronic pain equals objective cognitive fatigability.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Studies show subjective fatigue may correlate strongly with emotional distress while objective cognitive fatigability may not differ from controls.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Pain-related cognition effects are direct and independent of sleep/mood.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Sleep quality and affective factors partially mediate many associations.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Persistent pain state with features of central amplification or nociplastic pain.
Degree to which pain disrupts daily functioning and cognitive/emotional activity.
Sleep disruption attributable to pain symptoms.

research_gaps: Longitudinal multimodal designs integrating QST/neuroimaging, HRV/baroreflex, cortisol, sleep, and cognitive testing.
Temporal coupling between autonomic/HPA changes and daily brain fog/fatigue fluctuations.
Condition-specific comparisons across fibromyalgia, migraine, neuropathic pain, and chronic low back pain.

most_supported_patterns: Chronic centralized pain is associated with impaired attention/executive function/working memory.
Poor sleep quality and affective distress partially mediate pain-related cognition/fatigue.
Pain interference is often more informative than pain intensity for neurobehavioral outcomes.
patterns_requiring_caution: Central Sensitization Inventory should not be treated as direct proof of nociceptive sensitization.
Subjective brain fog/fatigue may exceed objective cognitive deficits.
Inflammatory/HPA biomarkers are inconsistent when measured at a single time point.
recommended_next_queries: Human evidence linking respiration, CO2, oxygen saturation, hyperventilation, panic, dizziness, fatigue, and cognition.
Human evidence linking blood pressure, orthostatic intolerance, hydration/electrolytes, dizziness, fatigue, and brain fog.
summary.overall_assessment: Strong graph value. Pain should be modeled as a multimodal stressor that affects sleep, autonomic regulation, affective load, fatigue, and selected cognitive domains. Do not model pain-to-cognition as a single direct deterministic edge.
